title: Finite Projected Gradient Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite projected-gradient bad projection and bad decrease QF_LRA/Farkas regressions
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact projection replay computes projected_x=1 for the interval [0,1], and exact objective replay computes projected decrease 3; malformed projected-point and decrease rows are inconsistent.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional projected-gradient rows only when they add distinct box/simplex projection, active-set, proximal, stochastic, or convergence pressure; keep general projected-gradient convergence and rate theorems in horizon lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode finite projected-gradient witnesses as deterministic exact-rational replay rows.
Replay derivatives, feasible interval bounds, unconstrained trial points, interval projections, objective values, and projected-step decrease over exact rationals.
Route false projected-feasibility and projected-decrease claims through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the projection boundary or objective decrease.
Keep general projected-gradient convergence, constraint qualifications, proximal variants, stochastic variants, and floating-point stability claims marked Lean-horizon until proof artifacts exist.
